note: seq=115: FIX backup.json clobber + run a real backup. Two writers raced: atlas_pgcopy_backup (new, writes .csv.gz + real backup.json) vs atlas-backup-status (old, counts *.dump=0 and republishes backup.json hourly -> clobbers the real result with retained_dumps:0). Disable atlas-backup-status.timer (the clobberer); pgcopy now OWNS backup.json. Run atlas-pgcopy-backup BLOCKING (capture rc/stderr) so if the COPY errors we SEE it. Its backup.json then shows the real retained_dumps + restore_tested (or the error).

steps.note: seq=115: stop backup.json clobber + run pgcopy backup blocking (capture errors).

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import subprocess as s,time; s.run(['systemctl','stop','atlas-backup-status.timer']); s.run(['systemctl','disable','atlas-backup-status.timer']); s.run(['systemctl','reset-failed','atlas-pgcopy-backup.service']); r=s.run(['systemctl','start','atlas-pgcopy-backup.service'],capture_output=True,text=True,timeout=3500); print('pgcopy backup rc',r.returncode,(r.stderr or '')[-200:])"
